HttpStatusCode.description: Hypertext Transfer Protocol (HTTP) response status codes.

CreateAttachmentParams.description: Parameters required for creating a new attachment via multipart form upload.

attachableType.description: The type of entity this attachment will be associated with.

attachmentType.description: The category/type of the attachment being uploaded.

id.description: The customer record id, primary key.

name.description:
The customer's name. This can be updated, but any PDF document created already
will not update with the new name. Future PDFs will be created using the updated
value though.

internalId.description:
Users can set an internal or custom ID value for a customer record.
This is helpful in integrating with other systems.

creatorId.description:
The user who created this customer info record.
Note that customer info records may also be automatically created by users as part of accepting a project invite.
In this case, the user who accepted the project invite would be the creator of this record.

name.description:
The contract name. Note this may be an empty string in some cases.
This name can be managed by either party (customer or contractor).
It is a shared record in this sense.

contractValue.description:
The contract value. Again, this value can be managed by both
the contractor and the customer.

ChangeNotificationContractor.description:
This respresents a recipient of a Change Notification. It also holds the response
to a Change Notification for a given contract.

noCostImpact.description:
This will be `true` if a contractor has responded with no cost impact
or if the customer has marked this contract as having no cost impact.

hasResponded.description: This will be `true` if there has been a response for this contract.

estimate.description: The customer managed estimated cost impact.

fileDownloadCount.description: The number of times the contractor has downloaded the attached files (if there were any attached).

contractId.description: The contract id between the contractor and customer, primary key.

contract.description: The associated contract for the response.

type.description:
The type of Change Notification will be either a "Pricing Request" or "Authorize T&M Work"
which may have opened T&M Tags for the contractor to use.

typeId.description:
The type is referenced by a numberic id.
`1 = Pricing Request`
`2 = Authorize T&M Work`

statusChangedAt.description: When the Change Notification status was last updated (customer view on "sent inbox" only).

description.description: The Change Notification description (visible on detailed view).

dateSubmitted.description:
The date the Change Notification was _originally_ submitted, visible to the customer only.
The contractor will see the date that they received the Change Notification which is different.
The date received comes from the Change Notification response record.

dateReceived.description:
The date the Change Notification was received (for "received inbox" only).
This is relative to each contractor making a request for Change Notifications.

costImpact.description: Actual calculated cost impact based on responses from contractors.

projectedCost.description: Projected cost impact based on estimate and actual costs from contractor responses.

totalSubmitted.description: The number of contractors the Change Notification was submitted to.

totalResponded.description: The number of contractors who have responded to the Change Notification.

contractors.description:
A list of all contractors who have been notified.
Note this list is only seen on the detailed view and is not included in the list Change Notifications
endpoint response. Additionally, only customers can see this list. Contractors do not see the other
contractors who were also included on the Change Notification.

response.description:
A single response only makes sense to be visible from the "received inbox," or the contractor's view.
This is the contractor's response to the Change Notification.

From the customer's view (or the "sent inbox") there is a 1:n relationship with the contractor
responses since a Change Notification may be sent to multiple contractors.

To see a list of all contractor responses, as a customer, use the endpoint to get a specific
Change Notification by id.

responses.description:
Responses from each contractor. This is only visible from the customer's view.

From the customer's view (or the "sent inbox") there is a 1:n relationship with the contractor
responses since a Change Notification may be sent to multiple contractors.

ChangeOrderRequestSummary.description:
An abbreviated Change Order Request object. This does not include all fields
or associated data.

ChangeNotificationResponse.description:
This holds the response to a Change Notification for a given contract.
It also includes an association to the Change Notification and if there
has been a response indicating a cost impact, it will also include
information about the associated Change Order Request.

noCostImpact.description:
This will be `true` if a contractor has responded with no cost impact
or if the customer has marked this contract as having no cost impact.

hasResponded.description: This will be `true` if there has been a response for this contract.

contractId.description: The contract id between the contractor and customer, primary key.

projectId.description: The contractor's project id, primary key.

cor.description:
When there is a response to a Change Notification indicating a cost impact,
this Change Order Request details what the cost impact is.

contract.description: The associated contract for the response.

firstName.description: The first name of the creator/user.

lastName.description: The last name of the creator/user.

ValidationError.description:
Note: This class is the same as defined by `express-validator` package.
It was just creating documentation issues with `tsoa` to use it from the package.

records.description:
An array of records or an empty array if none were found using the filtering options
or if none exist for the company.

count.description:
The total count of records. If there are more than the returned limit, additional
requests will need to be made to paginate through the results as needed.

Inbox.description:
For "log views" in Clearstory, this represents the direction of data.
These are also known as "inboxes."

ChangeNotificationSortFields.description:
Valid sorting fields for change notifications.
Note that the accepted sorting field values coming from request input are going to be uniform
and use camelCase. The actual values that will end up being needed can be camelCase or snake_case.
Consumers of the API will be able to rely upon just using camelCase field names this way.

domain.description:
The associated email domain for the company.
This helps identify other users who may be part of the same company upon sign up.

name.description:
The office name is only seen internally. It is not seen by other parties.
For example "Main" or "San Francisco" are common examples.

businessName.description:
An optional business name that will be used instead of the company name
throughout the application, in the context of projects from this office,
for other parties. So when a customer sees their contractor's company name,
it is derived from their contractor's project's office's business name if set.
Otherwise it will be the contractor's company name.

customId.description:
An internal or custom id users can assign their office.
This can help when integrating with other systems.

tzName.description:
The timezone the office is set to. This is important for PDF documents that get
generated, reporting, and more.

firstName.description: The first name of the creator/user.

lastName.description: The last name of the creator/user.

email.description: The user's email address (they also login with this).

roleId.description:
A basic role id, note that there are also more granular permissions used
by the system in certain areas.

`2 = Account Owner`
`3 = Admin`
`4 = Project Manager`
`5 = Field User`

roleName.description: A human friendly version of the user's basic role; Account Owner, Admin, Project Manager, or Field User.

offices.description: Users can belong to multiple offices within a company.

CompanyUserSortFields.description:
Valid sorting fields for change notifications.
Note that the accepted sorting field values coming from request input are going to be uniform
and use camelCase. The actual values that will end up being needed can be camelCase or snake_case.
Consumers of the API will be able to rely upon just using camelCase field names this way.

CompanyOfficeSortFields.description:
Valid sorting fields for change notifications.
Note that the accepted sorting field values coming from request input are going to be uniform
and use camelCase. The actual values that will end up being needed can be camelCase or snake_case.
Consumers of the API will be able to rely upon just using camelCase field names this way.

CompanyDivisionRecord.description:
There isn't much to a division, it's a value from a list of enumerated strings.
However, companies do configure which divisions they use. These company division records
indicate which are in use.

name.description:
The contract name. Note this may be an empty string in some cases.
This name can be managed by either party (customer or contractor).
It is a shared record in this sense.

contractValue.description:
The contract value. Again, this value can be managed by both
the contractor and the customer.

ContractSortFields.description:
Valid sorting fields for contracts.
Note that the accepted sorting field values coming from request input are going to be uniform
and use camelCase. The actual values that will end up being needed can be camelCase or snake_case.
Consumers of the API will be able to rely upon just using camelCase field names this way.

name.description: The actual label value that is displayed.

companyStandard.description:
If this is `true` then it means the label is seen by users in the company
when adding labels to things in the application. This is to allow a company
to prioritize an surface common labels. Users can create/add any label they like
to things. Since there's no restriction, this just helps make others aware
of important, common, or "standard" labels the company uses.

AssociatedTMTag.description: An abbreviated representation of a T&M Tag often used by associated parent objects.

id.description: The T&M Tag id, primary key

paddedTagNumber.description:
The T&M Tag number has a padded format for display, it begins with "EX" followed by 5 numbers with leading zeros.
This is automatically calculated and can't be set by users.

dateOfWorkPerformedAdditional.description: If there are any additional dates of work performed, they will be listed here.

totalHours.description: The sum of the labor hours on the T&M Tag.

ChangeOrderRequestSortFields.description: Sorting fields for change order requests.

premiumLaborTotal.description: The total number of combined premium time labor hours (premium over time and premium double time) associated with the Change Order Requests in this status.

laborTotal.description: The total number of combined straight/standard time, over time, and double time labor hours associated with the Change Order Requests in this status.

premiumDoubleTime.description: The total number of premium double time labor hours associated with the Change Order Requests in this status.

doubleTime.description: The total number of double time labor hours associated with the Change Order Requests in this status.

premiumOverTime.description: The total number of premium over time labor hours associated with the Change Order Requests in this status.

overTime.description: The total number of over time labor hours associated with the Change Order Requests in this status.

standardTime.description: The total number of straight or standard labor time hours associated with the Change Order Requests in this status.

status.description: The status system value (use these to pass to other endpoints when filtering for example).

valueCompleted.description: If using the "work in place tracking" feature, this is the percentage of total Change Order Request value completed in this status.

totalAmount.description: The sum dollar amount of Change Order Requests in this status.

totalCount.description: The total count of Change Order Requests in this status.

placeholder.description: Summary information for Change Order Requests in "Placeholder" status.

approved_co_issued.description: Summary information for Change Order Requests in "Approved CO Issued" status.

rejected.description: Summary information for Change Order Requests in "Void" (or "Rejected") status.

approved_to_proceed.description: Summary information for Change Order Requests in "Approved to Proceed" status.

in_review.description: Summary information for Change Order Requests in "In Review" status.

totalAmountRemaining.description: If using the "work in place tracking" feature, this is the total dollar value remaining for Change Order Requests.

valueCompleted.description: If using the "work in place tracking" feature, this is the total dollar value completed for Change Order Requests.

averageRequestedValue.description: The average requested value of Change Order Requests.

totalTags.description: The total number of associated T&M Tags.

totalStatusAmount.description: The total dollar amount of Change Order Requests (taking into consideration amount changes across all statuses).

totalRequestedAmount.description: The total original requested dollar amount of Change Order Requests.

totalCount.description: The total count of Change Order Requests.

rejected.description: The sum of the total amount for Change Order Requests in "Rejected" (or "Void") status

placeholder.description: The sum of the total amount for Change Order Requests in "Placeholder" status.

in_review.description: The sum of the total amount for Change Order Requests in "In Review" status.

approved_co_issued.description: The sum of the total amount for Change Order Requests in "Approved CO Issued" status.

approved_to_proceed.description: The sum of the total amount for Change Order Requests in "Approved to Proceed" status.

pendingContractValue.description: Adjusted sum of total contract value factoring in Change Order Requests.

originalContractValue.description: The sum of total contract values as manually input on each contract.

labelIds.description: A list of existing label IDs.

labels.description: A list of labels. If adding labels, new labels will be created if they do not already exist.

id.description: The customer record id, primary key.

name.description:
The customer's name. This can be updated, but any PDF document created already
will not update with the new name. Future PDFs will be created using the updated
value though.

internalId.description:
Users can set an internal or custom ID value for a customer record.
This is helpful in integrating with other systems.

creatorId.description:
The user who created this customer info record.
Note that customer info records may also be automatically created by users as part of accepting a project invite.
In this case, the user who accepted the project invite would be the creator of this record.

offices.description: The offices that this customer info record is available to be used by for projects.

creatorId.description: The creator id is a User id of a Company user (if not set, the user ID who owns the API key will be used)

internalId.description: An optional internal ID for your customer info record (this could be a reference to another system you use or other internal coding)

officeIds.description: A list of Clearstory Office IDs this customer info record should be available to be used by

state.description: The customer's state (prefer using US state abbreviation, but not required)

country.description: The customer's country (prefer using country code, but not required)

internalId.description: An optional internal ID for your customer info record (this could be a reference to another system you use or other internal coding)

state.description: The customer's state (prefer using US state abbreviation, but not required)

country.description: The customer's country (prefer using country code, but not required)

creatorId.description: The creator id is a User id of a Company user (if not set, the user ID who owns the API key will be used)

officeId.description: The Office id for the Project

customerId.description: Optional ID of a Clearstory Customer information record to associate to this project

customerInternalId.description: Optional Customer internal ID to associate a Clearstory customer information record to this project

jobNumber.description: The job number (your company's job number)

customerJobNumber.description: The customer's job number (your customer's job number if known)

contractValue.description: The contract value with your customer (up to two decimal places are supported)

state.description: The project's state (prefer using US state abbreviation, but not required)

country.description: The project's country (prefer using country code, but not required)

presetSupervisorNotes.description:
The project's preset supervisor notes (if different from the office's, this value will be used instead).
Note that this notes field, while often displayed in e-mails and PDFs, does not currently support HTML.
Please use text only. These are typically short notes in nature, but can support quite a bit of text.

contractName.description: The project's contract name (the contract with the customer)

customerId.description: Optional ID of a Clearstory Customer information record to associate to this project

jobNumber.description: The job number (your company's job number)

customerJobNumber.description: The customer's job number (your customer's job number if known)

contractValue.description: The contract value with your customer (up to two decimal places are supported)

state.description: The project's state (prefer using US state abbreviation, but not required)

country.description: The project's country (prefer using country code, but not required)

presetSupervisorNotes.description:
The project's preset supervisor notes (if different from the office's, this value will be used instead).
Note that this notes field, while often displayed in e-mails and PDFs, does not currently support HTML.
Please use text only. These are typically short notes in nature, but can support quite a bit of text.

division.description: The project division (note that this will not adjust any division default rates if changed).

internalId.description:
An internal or custom id users can assign to the rate item.
This is helpful when integrating with other systems.

rateGroupId.description: The rate group id, primary key.

standardItem.description:
If this is `true`, then the labor item will automatically be added
to the form when users create T&M Tags. The user will still need to
provide the quantity though. This is useful so user's don't forget
to add items that are common or standard.

These are not required, the user may also choose to remove the item
that was automatically added to the form upon creation. The items
will not come back if the user goes to update a draft that was previously
saved with the items removed.

internalId.description:
An internal or custom id users can assign to the rate item.
This is helpful when integrating with other systems.

rateGroupId.description: The rate group id, primary key.

standardItem.description:
If this is `true`, then the labor item will automatically be added
to the form when users create T&M Tags. The user will still need to
provide the quantity though. This is useful so user's don't forget
to add items that are common or standard.

These are not required, the user may also choose to remove the item
that was automatically added to the form upon creation. The items
will not come back if the user goes to update a draft that was previously
saved with the items removed.

projectRateId.description:
This is the project's labor rate record id. It has been returned in the response as
`projectRateId` to help remove confusion for which id to use when creating draft T&M Tags
and Change Order Requests.

companyRateId.description:
This is the original company level rate which this rate was created from, if it was
inherited from one. This can be null.

inheritCompanyRateChanges.description:
If true, then these rates will inherit chages from the company level. This is to say
that the project level rates have not deviated from the original rate group assigned.

projectRateId.description:
This is the project's material rate record id. It has been returned in the response as
`projectRateId` to help remove confusion for which id to use when creating draft T&M Tags
and Change Order Requests.

companyRateId.description:
This is the original company level rate which this rate was created from, if it was
inherited from one. This can be null.

inheritCompanyRateChanges.description:
If true, then these rates will inherit chages from the company level. This is to say
that the project level rates have not deviated from the original rate group assigned.

projectRateId.description:
This is the project's equipment rate record id. It has been returned in the response as
`projectRateId` to help remove confusion for which id to use when creating draft T&M Tags
and Change Order Requests.

companyRateId.description:
This is the original company level rate which this rate was created from, if it was
inherited from one. This can be null.

inheritCompanyRateChanges.description:
If true, then these rates will inherit chages from the company level. This is to say
that the project level rates have not deviated from the original rate group assigned.

projectRateId.description:
This is the project's other rate record id. It has been returned in the response as
`projectRateId` to help remove confusion for which id to use when creating draft T&M Tags
and Change Order Requests.

companyRateId.description:
This is the original company level rate which this rate was created from, if it was
inherited from one. This can be null.

inheritCompanyRateChanges.description:
If true, then these rates will inherit chages from the company level. This is to say
that the project level rates have not deviated from the original rate group assigned.

companyId.description: The id of the company who created the T&M Tag record.

creatorId.description: The id of the user who created the T&M Tag record.

paddedTagNumber.description: The T&M Tag "padded" or full number as displayed in app clients and PDF documents, ex. "EX00234"

totalHoursST.description: Sum of straight/standard time labor hours for the T&M Tag.

totalHoursOT.description: Sum of overtime time labor hours for the T&M Tag.

totalHoursDT.description: Sum of double time labor hours for the T&M Tag.

totalHoursPOT.description: Sum of premium overtime time labor hours for the T&M Tag.

totalHoursPDT.description: Sum of premium double time labor hours for the T&M Tag.

totalHours.description: Sum of all labor hours for the T&M Tag regardless of unit type.

TMTagLaborItem.description:
T&M Tag Labor items do not have rates associated with them.
However, if they relate to a labor rate that rate will be used when
a Change Order Request is made from the Tag. All items are a copy of a base rate
item and can carry their own customizations. So long as the `projectRateId` is referenced
the proper rate will be retrieved.

projectRateId.description:
If this item is associated with a project rate, the id must be set here. This is what ultimately is responsible
for retrieving the rate/cost when a Change Order Request is made. The rate/cost will not be displayed until the
item is used on a Change Order Request. Until then it appears as a line item with description, unit, quantity, etc.

If no `projectRateId` is provided here, the user will be prompted to provide a rate when going to create a Change
Order Request from a Tag with the item.

date.description: Each line item may carry an optional date

internalId.description: A custom internal id for your use

laborClass.description: The labor class or worker description

formGroupRowId.description:
Labor items are displayed ordered and grouped by matching `projectRateId` and `date` in the web and mobile app clients.
The default behavior of Extracker's app clients is to group matching labor items by `projectRateId`.

While this `formGroupRowId` value is required, it is not required to group labor line items. Each labor item may contain
a unique `formGroupRowId` value even if it has a `projectRateId` for the same date in common with another labor item.

The grouping is for user experience and compact visualization. You may not want each labor line item entered separately,
but it is within your control to decide with the public API.

The web app will allow users to drag and drop rows to re-order them, so be aware of changes in these values.

While technically required, the public API will attempt to set this value automatically if you do not provide one when
creating or updating a tag. It will be the next available number in order. However, you will lose the grouping functionality
if you do not explicitly provide this value.

TMTagMaterialItem.description:
T&M Tag Material items do not have rates associated with them.
However, if they relate to a material rate that rate will be used when
a Change Order Request is made from the Tag. All items are a copy of a base rate
item and can carry their own customizations. So long as the `projectRateId` is referenced
the proper rate will be retrieved.

projectRateId.description:
If this item is associated with a project rate, the id must be set here. This is what ultimately is responsible
for retrieving the rate/cost when a Change Order Request is made. The rate/cost will not be displayed until the
item is used on a Change Order Request. Until then it appears as a line item with description, unit, quantity, etc.

If no `projectRateId` is provided here, the user will be prompted to provide a rate when going to create a Change
Order Request from a Tag with the item.

date.description: Each line item may carry an optional date

internalId.description: A custom internal id for your use

TMTagEquipmentItem.description:
T&M Tag Equipment items do not have rates associated with them. However, if they relate to a equipment rate
that rate will be used when a Change Order Request is made from the Tag. All items are a copy of a base rate
item and can carry their own customizations. So long as the `projectRateId` is referenced the proper rate
will be retrieved.

projectRateId.description:
If this item is associated with a project rate, the id must be set here. This is what ultimately is responsible
for retrieving the rate/cost when a Change Order Request is made. The rate/cost will not be displayed until the
item is used on a Change Order Request. Until then it appears as a line item with description, unit, quantity, etc.

If no `projectRateId` is provided here, the user will be prompted to provide a rate when going to create a Change
Order Request from a Tag with the item.

date.description: Each line item may carry an optional date

internalId.description: A custom internal id for your use

TMTagOtherItem.description:
T&M Tag Other items do not have rates associated with them.
However, if they relate to an other rate that rate will be used when
a Change Order Request is made from the Tag. All items are a copy of a base rate
item and can carry their own customizations. So long as the `projectRateId` is referenced
the proper rate will be retrieved.

projectRateId.description:
If this item is associated with a project rate, the id must be set here. This is what ultimately is responsible
for retrieving the rate/cost when a Change Order Request is made. The rate/cost will not be displayed until the
item is used on a Change Order Request. Until then it appears as a line item with description, unit, quantity, etc.

If no `projectRateId` is provided here, the user will be prompted to provide a rate when going to create a Change
Order Request from a Tag with the item.

date.description: Each line item may carry an optional date

internalId.description: A custom internal id for your use

TMTagDetail.description:
The TMTagDetail object provides additional fields for the T&M Tag that the abbreviated
TMTag object does not provide.

companyId.description: The id of the company who created the T&M Tag record.

creatorId.description: The id of the user who created the T&M Tag record.

paddedTagNumber.description: The T&M Tag "padded" or full number as displayed in app clients and PDF documents, ex. "EX00234"

totalHoursST.description: Sum of straight/standard time labor hours for the T&M Tag.

totalHoursOT.description: Sum of overtime time labor hours for the T&M Tag.

totalHoursDT.description: Sum of double time labor hours for the T&M Tag.

totalHoursPOT.description: Sum of premium overtime time labor hours for the T&M Tag.

totalHoursPDT.description: Sum of premium double time labor hours for the T&M Tag.

totalHours.description: Sum of all labor hours for the T&M Tag regardless of unit type.

creatorId.description: The creator id is a user id of a company user

projectId.description: The project id for the T&M Tag

workDescription.description: The T&M Tag work description, this may contain HTML formatting

dateOfWorkPerformed.description:
At least one date of work performed is required, but this can be an array of dates.
When you retrieve a tag these are returned as two separate fields. There is a primary date
of work performed which is the _earliest_ date provided. This is ultimately what gets used
by users when sorting the T&M Tag log in the application. The additional dates are appended
on in various exports, e-mails, and are also visible in the T&M Tag log in the application.

emailTo.description: Who ths T&M Tag will be e-mailed to when submitted (your customer by default)

emailCC.description: Who will be CC'd on the T&M Tag submission e-mail (this is your company users by default)

workDescription.description: The T&M Tag work description, this may contain HTML formatting

dateOfWorkPerformed.description:
At least one date of work performed is required, but this can be an array of dates.
When you retrieve a tag these are returned as two separate fields. There is a primary date
of work performed which is the _earliest_ date provided. This is ultimately what gets used
by users when sorting the T&M Tag log in the application. The additional dates are appended
on in various exports, e-mails, and are also visible in the T&M Tag log in the application.

emailTo.description: Who ths T&M Tag will be e-mailed to when submitted (your customer by default)

emailCC.description: Who will be CC'd on the T&M Tag submission e-mail (this is your company users by default)

ClearstoryAPIKey.name: Authorization
ClearstoryAPIKey.description:
The Web API currently uses Basic Auth where the username is the _Key ID_ and password is the _Key Secret._ **These API keys provide access to your data at a company level.**

There is a limit to the number of API keys a given company can have generated and active at any given time. Keys can be disabled and created again.

It is important to note the _Secret Key_ value cannot be retrieved again. Should you lose it, you will need to generate a new API key.

Basic Auth works simply by including an `Authorization` header with a base64 encoded string value that combines your _**Key ID**_ and _**Key Secret**_ separated by a colon. ie. `476B2524694742F2B2ACA15ACE3518C9:112cd32ec0d015fe9c5709d14cef3201`.

Your final header should look something like: `Authorization: Basic YWRzZmFkZmFrZmpoM2sybGpma3dhMw==`

info.title: Clearstory Web API
info.description:
The Clearstory Web API allows you to access and build your own applications that utilize your Clearstory data. The Clearstory Web API provides a RESTful interface with JSON-formatted responses to access many Clearstory resources. Check back here for more information as we continue to expanding our API functionality.

Web API domain: `https://web-api.clearstory.build`

200.description: Promise resolving to the created attachment details

post.description: Creates a new attachment by uploading a file to S3 storage.

requestBody.description: - The attachment creation parameters including file data and metadata

schema.description: - The attachment creation parameters including file data and metadata

get.description:
Retrieves a Change Notification sent by the requesting (customer) company.
Contractors will use the `/change-notifications/:id/:contractId` endpoint
to retrieve information about the Change Notification received in relation
to one of their contracts that they have with their customer.

Change Notifications have a 1:n relationship with contractor responses.
The same Change Notification could be sent to the same contractor under
one or more contracts. When a contractor is responding to Change Notifications
they will need to specify for which contract.
Retrieves a list of Change Notifications either sent or received by the company belonging to the requesting API token.

parameters.description: How many records to skip (can use also "skip")
parameters.name: offset

parameters.description: How many records to take (can also use "take")
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: A list of project ids (or internal ids) to filter results by
parameters.name: projectIds

parameters.description: A list of office ids to filter results by
parameters.name: officeIds

parameters.description: A list of internal office ids (user managed) to filter results by
parameters.name: internalOfficeIds

get.description:
Retrieves the divisions being used by the company belonging to the requesting API token.

Note: Division values are restricted to an enumerated list of strings. Users and projects may
optionally be associated with a division, but this value is not free-form and this association
is not an actual database relationship. It is more of a label, but can change application behavior.

There are various filters in the web application UI for division and default rates may be
assigned to divisions to use on newly created projects instead of an office's default rates.

It would be uncommon to see a very long list of divisions returned from this endpoint.

parameters.description: How many records to skip (can use also "skip")
parameters.name: offset

parameters.description: How many records to take (can also use "take")
parameters.name: limit

API is accessed using an API key that is not related to any user and acts more like a service account.

parameters.description: Filter to include CORs by date submitted starting from (greater than or equal to) this ISO8601 date, ie. `"2022-01-01"` or `"2022-01-01T00:03:00Z"` or `"2022-01-01T00:03:00-8:00"`. Note: all dates are stored in UTC, so it is recommended that you express your dates this way. If you do not specify a timezone, UTC will be used. For example `"2022-01-26"` will be treated as `"2022-01-26T00:00:00Z"`

post.description:
Creates a new customer info record.

Note: You may set an "internal ID" for this Customer record that relates to another system or other
internal coding system you may have. This is optional, but it can help you better relate and identify
customer information records internally.

When you create a new Project, you can pass either the Clearstory ID for the Customer information record
OR your own internal ID to associate/set the customer information with the project.

Also note that you must pass a list of Clearstory Office IDs since Customer info records are associated
with Offices. Each Office in your Company could have a different Customer info record with the same
customer name, but different address. When you create a new Project, the given Customer info record
must also belong to the same Office that the Project is being created in.

get.description:
Retrieves an individual customer info record given its id.
You can use this id to associate this information to a Project when creating a Project.

Note: Your customer does not need a Clearstory account. This information is owned and used by you for
your needs in the web application for display and filtering your data. It is also used for display
in PDF documents that you create using Clearstory. For example, address information on T&M Tags
and Change Order Requests that your company sends to your customer.

parameters.description: The customer info record id
parameters.name: id

value.message: there was a problem with the request

patch.description:
Updates a Customer information record.

These records can associated to multiple projects (and across different offices, these are used company-wide),
so you can easily and quickly update customer information on multiple projects at the same time.

patch.description:
Updates a project.

Note: You can't change the Office a project belongs to. There are many considerations if this operation is required such as users
who may or may not belong to an Office. Also note that the Customer info records are associated with offices and so the customer
info's office association may also need to change.

Associated Customer info records can be updated as well, but the Customer info record must be associated with the project's office
otherwise it will not update the association (and no error will be returned).

get.description:
Retrieves a list of LMEO rates for a given project id. Project rates are separate records which are inherited from
company level rates (still grouped by a rate group). So long as a project does not alter its rates, these rates will
continue to receive updates from the company level set. However, once a project deviates from the company level group
it will no longer receive rate updates. This is by rate type; so if a project begins customizing its labor rates but
has not changed anything about its equipment rates, the equipment rates will continue to receive updates.

This permits a flexible system where projects can't pollute rates for one another or the entire company, yet still
enjoy a synchronization if the user wishes to steer from the company level and never customize rates at the project
level.

How can you tell if a project level rate continues to receive updates? The response for this endpoint will return
a field `inheritCompanyRateChanges` and if this is true, then rate values will continue to be updated as the company
level rate updates.

parameters.description: Which rate type
parameters.name: rateType

parameters.name: skip

parameters.description: How many records to take
parameters.name: take

parameters.name: sortBy

parameters.description: The sorting direction
parameters.name: sortDirection

parameters.name: internalIds

post.description:
Creates a new T&M Tag draft. T&M Tags have ownership and therefore a user must be provided, normally this would be the requesting user,
but from the public API you will need choose the user. Specify this with the `creatorId` field in the request body.

The other associated object you'll need is a project for which you will need to know the project id for `projectId`.

There are customers and company users that get added to the e-mail list for each tag when it is submitted. This will be automatically
done when creating a draft Tag from the public API. However, you can control what these emails are by providing a list of emails
for `emailTo` and `emailCC` respectively. If you do not include these keys in your JSON request payload then they will be automatically
added. If you provide empty arrays or null, it will _not_ set e-mails for the T&M Tag (respective to each; To/CC).

LMEO (labor, material, equipment, other) items for the Tag can be added as well. These key values will replace the existing items
when updating a Tag. Each has a slightly different, but similar set of required fields. While T&M Tags carry no information about
pricing/rates, these LMEO items _can_ relate back to a LMEO rate item from the project's rate group. This is the `projectRateId` field
under each LMEO item object and it is optional. If no rate is associated, when the T&M Tag is used to create a Change Order Request,
the user will be prompted to provide a value. To avoid manual data entry, it is certainly preferably to pull from associate rates,
but it is also possible to have an item that simply does not have a company rate.

While LMEO items pull from a list of project level rates, their descriptions can be changed or customized. This provides additional
flexibility to make adjustments on an adhoc basis yet retain the original associated rate.

_Note that currently attaching files from the public API is not supported._

patch.description:
Updates a T&M Tag draft, modifying only the fields given. Note that you must pass all existing T&M Tag items
(labor, material, equipment, other) if providing the keys in the JSON request body. Otherwise it will remove
those items. However, if the key is omitted, it will not wipe out the items. It is a PATCH method in this sense,
but there's no quick way to add just one more labor item. All existing labor items would need to be added along
with the new one.

Also note that there are some field which can't be updated such as the project id and creator (user) id.

get.description: Retrieves an individual T&M Tag given its id.

parameters.description: The tag id
parameters.name: id

delete.description: Removes an individual tag given its id.

parameters.description: The tag id
parameters.name: id

tags.name: LMEO Rates
tags.description: Labor, material, equipment, and other rates.
